SnowDistribution: A day of flat light and no definition. Winds 20 -30 mph. Very hard snow ice on exposed windward slopes.
Comment: A couple of dry and bright days are forecast. Expect icy paths.
WeatherInfluences: Overnight snow will clear to leave a dry and bright day. The freezing level will hover around 300 metres. Very strong West-North-Westerly winds will steadily veer North and decrease throughout the day to become light.
SnowStability: Weaknesses within existing windslab will persist and fresh unstable accumulations will build, both in wind-sheltered terrain above 850 metres. Aspects most affected are North-West through North to South-East with isolated new windslab deposits on Southerly aspects later. Fragile cornices above the same aspects. Wind exposed slopes will be firm and icy with a fresh dusting of snow on top. The avalanche hazard will be Considerable.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: Drier for a brief period in the morning before afternoon showers, falling as snow to 400 metres. The freezing level was around 700 metres initially, decreasing rapidly throughout the day. Fresh North-Westerly winds.
ObservedSnowStability: The existing windslab has started to consolidate, although weaknesses are still present, primarily above 900 metres on North-West to East aspects. Fresh deposits of unstable windslab are starting to develop in wind-sheltered locations, mostly on East and South-East aspects. On wind exposed slopes, a fresh dusting of new snow lies on top of a firm and icy base. The avalanche hazard is Considerable.

SnowDistribution: Steep, good snow ice conditions, mostly icy at higher elevations.  Clear in the morning with poor visibility in clouds and snow afternoon.  Winds 20 -30 mph progress difficult at times with stronger gusts
Comment: Good snow cover, with a wider distribution of new unstable windslab developing overnight.
WeatherInfluences: The freezing level is expected to be sub 500 metres throughout. Snowfall overnight but easing to give a bright day. Winds will be gale force from the North-West overnight, veering and easing during  to become light Northerlies by afternoon.
SnowStability: Snowfall with redistribution of existing deposits will see new moderately to weakly bonded windslab developing on North-East through South-East to South-West aspects above 900 metres. Instabilities will also persist within deep deposits on West to North aspects. Gully tops, scarp slopes and corrie rims will be worst affected. Instabilities will be encountered at lower levels in steep sheltered terrain. Exposed slopes will be scoured and icy.   The avalanche hazard will be Considerable.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: A bright start to the day but becoming cloudy around the tops early afternoon. Around -2 degrees at 900 metres for most of the day. Winds, mainly North-West but veering to the North and strengthening to gale force.
ObservedSnowStability: Moderately to weakly bonded windslab is present in wind sheltered locations on West through North to North-East aspects above 900 metres. Steep slopes, corrie rims and gully tops are worst affected. Fragile cornices and wind features have formed. Moderately bonded windslab is present below this altitude and on some South-West and East aspects. Wind exposed slopes are scoured and icy.  The avalanche hazard is Considerable.

SnowDistribution: Good snow cover on all aspects. Poor visibility. Light to moderate winds.
Comment: Some brighter weather forecast for Tuesday, Wednesday and Thursday.
WeatherInfluences: Strong North-Westerly winds in the evening veer to the North-North-East and then ease during the day. Snow showers in the evening followed by a dry day. Freezing level around 300 metres.
SnowStability: Unstable windslab will persist mainly on North to East aspects above 900 metres.  Fresh unstable windslab will also develop, mainly overnight, on South-East to South-West aspects above 900 metres. At lower elevations stability will be better. Recently formed cornices will be fragile. The avalanche hazard will be Considerable.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: Light to moderate winds veered from the South-West to the North-West during the day. Snow showers. The freezing level was around 600 metres at midday and dropping.
ObservedSnowStability: Fresh areas of poorly bopnded windslab are present mainly on North to East aspects above 900 metres. Fresh cornices above these aspects are unstable. Below 900 metres the snowpack stability is better. Other aspects are generally firm and icy. The avalanche hazard is Considerable.

SnowDistribution: Fresh snow on most aspects overlying the existing hard snow ice and icy paths. No visibility throughout the day above 600 metres. Moderate summit winds.
Comment: High pressure building bringing dry and largely settled conditions.
WeatherInfluences: Snowfall will continue through the evening, heaviest around midnight, before clearing to leave a dry day. Strong winds from the North-West will increase to gale force for a few hours overnight. These will gradually ease, veering becoming more from the North. The freezing level will fluctuate around 300 metres.
SnowStability: Moderately to weakly bonded windslab will persist on North-West to North-East aspects above 900 metres. Localised areas of moderately bonded windslab will continue to build on East to South aspects. Gully tops, crag aprons and coire rims will be most affected. Unstable cornices will remain a threat. Below this height, instabilities will be found in steep wind sheltered locations. Wind exposed areas will be stable, firm and icy. The avalanche hazard will be Considerable.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: It was a dry morning with snow falling during the afternoon. Strong winds from the North-West with the freezing level around 600 metres at noon.
ObservedSnowStability: Areas of unstable windslab are present on mainly North-West to East aspects above 900 metres. Unstable cornices remain a hazard on these same aspects. Below this altitude, the snow pack is slowly starting to consolidate but weaknesses remain in steep wind sheltered locations such as river beds and convexities. The avalanche hazard is Considerable.

SnowDistribution: Snow cover above 400 metres with deeper accumulations and drifted snow above 800 metres. West to North-Westerly winds approx 10-20 mph at summit level calmer at lower altitudes. Some poor visibility at times above 800 metres.
Comment: High pressure developing bringing dry and largely settled conditions with light to moderate winds. Remaining cold, freezing level around between 200-300 metres.
WeatherInfluences: The freezing level falling to around 300 metres with new overnight snow falling on strong North-Westerly winds. Drier conditions are expected through the rest of the forecast period and winds will ease veering to become more Easterly direction by the end of the day..
SnowStability: Areas of weakly bonded windslab will persist with new deposits developing especially in wind sheltered locations. Greatest accumulations will be above 850m particularly in sheltered gullies and on steep slopes with a N to SE aspect. Fragile cornices will also exist above similar areas. Cross loading may affect some other wind sheltered aspects. Wind exposed slopes the stability will generally be better with a light cover of new snow on a firmer base. The avalanche hazard will be Considerable.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: New snow fell overnight mainly above 700 metres. This morning was predominately dry with light to moderate West to North-Westerly winds. After the cool night the freezing level rose to around 800 metres. Visibility came and went, the high summits were generally cloud covered with the sun trying to break through.
ObservedSnowStability: Areas of weakly bonded windslab exists in wind sheltered gullies, hollows and steep slopes mainly on North to Easterly aspects above 900 metres with gully exits particularly affected. Cross loading has affected other wind sheltered aspects but to a slightly lesser extent. On exposed slopes a light cover of new snow exists on a firm and icy base. The avalanche hazard is Considerable.

SnowDistribution: Deep snow at higher elevations on many aspects.  Blizzard and whiteout conditions presenting challenging and serious conditions. Winds 20 -30 mph progress difficult at times with stronger gusts.
WeatherInfluences: Fresh to strong North-Westerly winds this evening with further snowfall above 250 metres. Snow showers to lower elevations for the remainder of the night and through the morning as the Northerly winds ease. Dryer in the afternoon with light to moderate winds.
SnowStability: Further deposits of unstable windslab will accumulate tonight and through the morning. Significant accumulations becoming more wide spread will be found in steep locations on mainly East to South aspects above 800 metres though other aspects and elevations will also be affected. Cornices will be fragile in these locations also. Areas exposed to the wind will be firm and generally stable. The avalanche hazard will be Considerable.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: Prolonged showers falling as snow above around 300 meters this morning, easing with brighter intervals during the afternoon. Winds have been fresh to strong from North or North-Westerly directions.
ObservedSnowStability: There are fresh accumulations of new and wind blown snow on many aspects and to lower levels. Above 650 metres these accumulations are gaining depth on top of a generally firm and stable existing snowpack. At higher elevations, above 850 metres, unstable wind slab is present in steep wind sheltered locations mainly on East to South aspects with other aspects affected through variable winds. Cornices are fragile in these locations also. The avalanche hazard is Considerable.
